attacking_style: Attacks from breakaways with significant distance remaining (18km observed), then extends advantage on final climbs through time trial power. Prefers long-range moves requiring sustained power rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: Primarily functions as elevated domestique with chase/tempo duties for UAE, but given freedom in breakaway scenarios. Limited evidence of dedicated leadout support; tactical role flexible based on race situation.
terrain_preference: Hybrid stages favoring TT specialists with climbing components. Excels when heat and distance create attrition. Final climbs suit his ability to grind power rather than punchy/steep gradients.
vulnerability: Crashes significantly compromise his race effectiveness due to high power expenditure required to rejoin. Stage role becomes compromised when recovering from incidents, suggesting limited explosive reserves.
key_patterns: Time trial power translates to climbing resilience on long, hot stages (2 observations)
Effective in extended chase/tempo efforts for team, demonstrating domestique versatility (2 observations)
Breakaway success dependent on solo time trialing ability after attacking from groups (2 observations)
Stage disruptions via crashes disproportionately affect his performance capacity (observed weakness)
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 showed evolution from pure domestique to stage-hunting capability, winning from breakaway with climbing resilience that exceeded TT specialist expectations
